description: OR-set / LWW liveness cells on the CrdtSync plane (protocol.md § Reliable Sync, #lzsync-liveness). Cross-process editor liveness — '(doc, pid) is open' and 'pid alive' / owner lease — is carried as CRDT cells, not a bespoke frame, so it inherits idempotent, frontier-resumable, re-delivery-safe convergence (proven ReliableSync.crdt_liveness_convergence_under_retry, building on the CRDT lattice proofs). Open-set membership is an observed-remove set (add wins over a concurrent stale remove); per-pid `alive` and the lease are HLC-stamped LWW registers. Derived authority 'doc Y is live' = any present (doc,pid) whose alive[pid] is true — a derived aggregate over the liveness family (#lzfamilysync). One alive[pid]=false fans out to every doc that pid held (whole-editor-death cascade). Keys are namespaced by document_hash so a stale overlay for doc B cannot flip doc A. Ops ride schemas/distributed.json (CrdtSync); this fixture pins the derived liveness aggregate, not a new frame.

scenarios.name: open_set_add_wins_over_stale_remove
scenarios.description: OR-set observed-remove: a re-open (add tag t3) concurrent with a lagging close (remove observing only the earlier add tag t1) keeps the doc open. The remove shadows only the adds it observed.

scenarios.name: lww_alive_highest_stamp_wins
scenarios.description: Per-pid alive is a LWW register: the OS process-exit write (alive=false at a higher stamp) wins; a stale re-assert (alive=true at a lower stamp) is dominated.

scenarios.name: whole_editor_death_cascades
scenarios.description: pid 100 has docA and docB open. One alive[100]=false write recomputes the derived live aggregate for BOTH docs to not-live — the derived slot fans out over every (doc,pid) the pid held. Per-doc close stays a separate open-set remove.

expect.note: docA and docB both drop live because pid100 died; docC (pid200) unaffected

scenarios.name: derived_live_doc_aggregate_converges_under_retry
scenarios.description: Two replicas exchange the same liveness ops in different orders and with re-delivery; the derived per-doc live aggregate converges identically (semilattice join). Re-ingesting a seen op applies 0 new ops.
